name: Full-Stack AI Engineer Rubric
audience: engineers bridging product UI and agent/backend workflows
weeklyTheme: Make end-to-end AI product proof concrete and recruiter-readable.

signals.label: Target role is explicit
signals.gap: The current positioning does not make the AI/full-stack target obvious.
signals.nextAction: Rewrite the positioning line around the full-stack AI role you want.

signals.label: Enough public proof exists
signals.gap: There are not yet enough proof artifacts to support the role shift.
signals.nextAction: List at least three proof artifacts that span UI, backend, or agent workflows.

signals.label: Public proof surfaces are linked
signals.gap: GitHub is not yet linked as a proof surface.
signals.nextAction: Link GitHub and connect it to a portfolio or case-study surface.

signals.label: Retrieval or tool workflows are visible
signals.gap: The proof list does not yet show retrieval or tool-backed product workflows clearly.
signals.nextAction: Publish or rename proof artifacts so retrieval and tool flows are visible.

signals.label: Both frontend and backend depth are visible
signals.gap: The skill mix does not yet show enough end-to-end engineering depth.
signals.nextAction: List the strongest skills across UI, APIs, architecture, and system design.

signals.label: System-design narrative exists
signals.gap: There is not yet enough visible system-design narrative for interview rounds.
signals.nextAction: Write one architecture note and one STAR story about end-to-end tradeoffs.

signals.label: Claims and limitations are explicit
signals.gap: Claims and mock boundaries are not yet explicit enough.
signals.nextAction: Add constraints that define what is real, mocked, or planned.

signals.label: Case-study surface exists
signals.gap: No portfolio or case-study surface is linked yet.
signals.nextAction: Add a portfolio or long-form project page for the flagship proof artifact.

projectBlueprints.title: Agent Workflow Console
projectBlueprints.problem: Most portfolios show either backend pipelines or frontend polish, not the operator-facing AI workflow between them.
projectBlueprints.solution: Build a console that shows user prompt, retrieval, tool execution, approval, and output states end to end.
projectBlueprints.recruiterTakeaway: Shows the candidate can reason across product, backend, and observable AI workflows.
interviewTalkingPoints: How UI and backend contracts were shaped

readmeChecklist: Explain the system from user input to final output.
Make mocked and real integrations explicit.
Show one architecture diagram or visual.
Add setup instructions that work locally.
truthfulnessGuardrails: Do not imply production scale unless proven.
Separate shipping evidence from roadmap ideas.
positioningComparison: Use occupation-ops to build end-to-end AI proof before outreach or applications.
Use job-search ops tools when the application workflow itself becomes the bottleneck.
